name: Write-Time Keyed Slots with Contrastive Address Pretext (KeyedWM)
core_idea: Replace address-by-value with explicit write-time keys: at every position store BOTH a separate addressing KEY k_t=W_k(h_t) and a content VALUE v_t=W_v(h_t) into the slot, instead of re-deriving the match vector from the content. Reads score q_p . k_buf (query vs stored KEY) and retrieve v_buf (content) — a true key/value separation like a hash table. To kill the credit-assignment chicken-and-egg, add a self-supervised CONTRASTIVE ADDRESS PRETEXT loss (InfoNCE) that trains q/k to point read-queries at the slot that is the correct retrieval target, with a target derived for free from same-document write-positions, so the addressing function receives strong gradient EVEN WHEN the DeltaNet recurrent shortcut already solves the LM token.
why_reliable: Two structural fixes to the two diagnosed failures. (1) Address-by-value/magnitude degeneracy is removed by giving the key its own projection W_k that is L2-normalized before scoring (cosine address) — score geometry no longer scales with content norm, so a small-norm value can no longer flatten the softmax; W_v is free to carry content at any magnitude. (2) The chicken-and-egg is broken by the contrastive pretext: addressing is no longer supervised ONLY through the LM loss (which DeltaNet shortcuts at ~0.5 recall, starving the read of gradient). The pretext directly maximizes log-softmax of the correct-slot score at every read position regardless of whether the read changes the LM logit, so q/k get dense, always-on gradient from step 0 and converge to a sharp matcher BEFORE the read needs to be load-bearing. The read injection still uses the zero-init read_alpha/floor escape hatch, so a not-yet-useful read can never hurt the LM loss while the pretext trains the geometry underneath.
why_generalizes: The pretext target is not symbol identity (query token == stored token) — that would only work on MQAR. It is 'the read-query at position p should retrieve the write-key of the position whose CONTENT the model needed', which we instantiate generically as: for each read position, the positive slot is the most recent same-document write position whose hidden state has the highest gradient-relevance to the current prediction (approximated cheaply by teacher target: the write position whose value most reduces next-token loss, computed once per batch and detached). On natural code/agentic data this trains semantic addressing: a call site's query learns to match the def's stored key; a variable use matches its binding; a sub-goal matches the relevant earlier tool-output — because the key W_k(h) encodes the SEMANTIC content of the hidden state, not the token id. Cosine keys + a learned key projection are exactly what lets 'find the def for this call' be a smooth, learnable retrieval rather than an exact-match lookup. Multi-hop falls out because reads can be chained across think steps, each refining the query from the previously-retrieved value.
concrete_changes: WorkingMemory.__init__ (experiments/model.py ~997): add self.W_k = nn.Linear(d_model, d_mem, bias=False) (init std 0.02). Keep W_v as content-only. Optionally add self.key_norm = True to L2-normalize keys and queries before scoring (cosine address, temperature-scaled).

WorkingMemory.forward (~1072): compute k=self.W_k(h); gather buf_k alongside buf_v via the same top_idx; change scores = einsum('btd,bkd->btk', F.normalize(q,-1), F.normalize(buf_k,-1)) / temp + log(buf_g). Retrieval read = einsum(attn, buf_v) unchanged. Keep causal/doc/pad masks identical. Stash buf_k for the probe.

Add compute_wm_address_pretext_loss(model, h, read_mask, doc_ids, targets) helper: for read positions, build positive-slot index from the teacher rule (most-loss-reducing same-doc write position, computed under no_grad with a single extra value-substitution forward OR the cheap proxy: nearest same-doc prior position whose token == answer for synthetic / highest W_v.W_v similarity for natural text), then InfoNCE over the slot scores. Add it to the loss in sft_code.py and train_lm.py behind --wm_address_pretext_weight (default 0.0).

forward_step decode path (_wm_append_one ~2872, _wm_read_one ~2899): append buf['key']=W_k(h) alongside buf['value']; score against buf['key'] with the same normalize+temp. Keeps incremental decode consistent with full-seq forward.

Checkpoint compat: W_k is a new param; old ckpts load with strict=False. To stay byte-identical at cold load, optionally init W_k = W_v.clone() so step-0 behaviour reproduces address-by-value, then the pretext pulls them apart.
probe_wm_utilization.py: report key-vs-value cosine separation and pretext accuracy alongside val_hit/top_mass.
validation: Synthetic probe FIRST: run probe_wm_utilization.py on saturated MQAR (d=64,L=2,T=512,K=128) across the same 5 seeds that gave 0.51..0.999. Success = (a) variance collapses (all seeds > 0.9 recall), (b) val_hit rate jumps from ~0.005 to near 1.0 and top_mass from 0.156 to >0.9 on the previously-failing seeds, (c) the pretext loss drives val_hit even when read_alpha is floored near 0 early (proving addressing learns before the read is load-bearing). Ablate: pretext-off (keyed-only) vs keyed+pretext to isolate which fix matters. Path to real model: add --wm_address_pretext_weight 0.05 to a short SFT continuation on the Phase-C base, then measure eval_longctx_recall.py (does semantic recall improve at distance 256-512?) and the def-lookup / type-recall agentic probes; confirm read_alpha grows and HumanEval does not regress.
risks: The teacher rule for the positive slot on NATURAL text is the weakest link — a bad target trains the wrong geometry. Mitigate by starting the pretext only on synthetic memory tasks (where the target is exact) and the longctx_recall data, then transferring; on free code use the cheap value-similarity proxy and keep the weight small.

Extra forward for the teacher target (loss-reduction-per-slot) is O(K) value substitutions; expensive. Use the cheap nearest-write proxy or batch-subsample positions (mirror process_reward's --max_positions).
InfoNCE over K=mem_size slots can be unstable if many near-duplicate keys exist (homogeneous think bursts) — temperature and key-norm help, but may need a diversity term like PKM FIX 5.
Cosine-normalizing the query removes magnitude as a 'how confident' signal; the log(buf_g) write-gate bias must carry that, so verify the gate still modulates retrieval strength.
Two pathways now compete for capacity (LM read + pretext); if the pretext weight is too high VAL ppl could drift (same trade-off seen with process_reward). Sweep the weight.
novelty: Key/value separation in attention is standard; storing an EXPLICIT write-time key in a bounded linear-RNN side-buffer (vs re-deriving the match from content at read time) is the structural change here and is not what the current WM does. The genuinely novel piece is the CONTRASTIVE ADDRESS PRETEXT that supervises the retrieval geometry independently of the LM loss to defeat the recurrent-shortcut credit-assignment trap — this is not in PKM (which uses epsilon-greedy + diversity to spread slot usage, a different failure) nor in standard kNN-LM/Memorizing-Transformer (which assume exact-key kNN, no learned semantic address under a no-KV-cache bound). Closest prior art: retrieval-pretraining / REALM-style retriever warmup and InfoNCE memory addressing, but applied here specifically to bootstrap a bounded-state linear-RNN's working memory addressing.
